author: sgbihu
title: CachingTest LoadMultiWithConfig_race import_model call count mismatch on Windows
signature: Actual function call count doesnt match EXPECT_CALL import_model Expected called times Actual called times unsatisfied and active|Flaky Test
failed_jobs: C++ unit tests / C++ unit tests
key_errors: caching_test.cpp(2667): error: Actual function call count doesn't match EXPECT_CALL(*mockPlugin, import_model(A<std::istream&>(), _))... Expected: to be called 7 times. Actual: called 6 times - unsatisfied and active
caching_test.cpp(312): error: Value of: Mock::VerifyAndClearExpectations(mockPlugin.get()) Actual: false Expected: true
root_cause: A race condition in the CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race test caused a non-deterministic import_model call count. The test expects exactly 7 calls to import_model during multi-device parallel loading with config, but under timing variation on the Windows runner, only 6 calls occurred. This is a flaky test related to thread scheduling on the CI runner, not a code correctness issue in the PR under test (PR #36488 adds profiling start_time for CPU/GPU/NPU and does not modify caching logic).
reproduction_steps: Run CachingTest.LoadMultiWithConfig_race/ByModelName_testCache repeatedly on Windows to observe intermittent import_model count mismatch.
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed CI job - the failure is flaky/transient.
Consider marking the race test with a retry policy or FLAKY annotation in the test suite.
Investigate the EXPECT_CALL count assumption in caching_test.cpp line 2667 for sensitivity to thread scheduling.
